main.270: GYUTRON verbindet Smart-Kameras, Weißlicht-Vision-Beleuchtung, Industriesensoren, Barcode-Verifikation, Edge Control und Qualitätsdaten-Workflows zu Prüfsystemen, die Fehler früher erkennen und nachgelagerte Prozesse schützen.
main.272: Entwickelt für Inline-Prüfung, Montageverifikation, Rückverfolgbarkeit und reproduzierbare Qualitätsnachweise über Produktionslinien hinweg.
main.278: Von Sichtprüfungen bis zu rückverfolgbaren Qualitätsentscheidungen.
main.279: Inspiriert von fortschrittlichen industriellen Prüf-Ökosystemen positioniert diese Lösung GYUTRON als mehr als einen Hardwarelieferanten: Kameras, Sensorik, Codelesen, Beleuchtung, Controller und Qualitätsaufzeichnungen werden als ein operatives System geplant.
main.280: Fehlteile, falsche Ausrichtung, Montagefehler, Oberflächenprobleme, Höhenabweichungen und sichtbare Defekte im Produktionstakt erkennen.
main.281: Barcodes und DPM-Codes lesen, Etiketten validieren, Scandaten mit Chargen verknüpfen und Prüfnachweise jeder Einheit zuordnen.
main.282: Kalibrierte Bildgebung, Lasermessung und Prüfgeräte für wiederholbare Toleranz- und Oberflächenprüfungen einsetzen.
main.283: Roboterbewegung, feste Bildverarbeitung und geführte Prüfpunkte für Endmontage, Verpackung und mehrseitige Produktkontrolle kombinieren.
main.285: Prüf-Hardware, die mit Fabrikentscheidungen vernetzt ist.
main.286: Die Architektur verbindet Bildaufnahme, Sensortrigger, Beleuchtungssteuerung, Edge-Analyse, PLC-Handshakes und Qualitätsdatenerfassung, sodass Prüfergebnisse Fehler stoppen, bevor sie nachgelagert weiterlaufen.
main.289: Smart-Kameras, Codeleser, Sensoren und stabile Weißlichtbeleuchtung erfassen zuverlässige Daten direkt am Prüfpunkt.
main.291: Edge-Controller und Vision-Tools bewerten Anwesenheit, Position, Maße, Codequalität und Oberflächenzustände.
main.293: PLC-Signale, I/O-Module und Roboter- oder Förderlogik können Einheiten je nach Prüfergebnis ausschleusen, stoppen, umleiten oder freigeben.
main.295: Bilder, Ergebnisse, Geräteereignisse und Scan-IDs lassen sich für MES, WMS, Dashboards oder Kundenqualitätsberichte strukturieren.
main.297: Ein praxisnaher Weg von der Pilotstation zum wiederholbaren Linieneinsatz.
main.298: Die Lösungsarbeit von GYUTRON sollte vom Produktionsproblem ausgehen und dann Gerätewahl, Prüflogik, Datenschnittstellen und operative Übergabe in einer kontrollierten Abfolge festlegen.
main.300: Fehlertypen, Liniengeschwindigkeit, Musterteile, Ausschlusslogik, Rückverfolgbarkeitsdaten und Pass/Fail-Verantwortung klären.
main.301: Kameras, Beleuchtung, Sensoren, Scanner, Edge-Controller, Montage und Schnittstellen rund um die physische Station auswählen.
main.303: Pilotprüfungen durchführen, Schwellenwerte abstimmen, Wiederholgenauigkeit dokumentieren und dieselbe Architektur für benachbarte Stationen vorbereiten.
main.304: Planen Sie eine Prüfstation mit GYUTRON.
main.305: Teilen Sie Bauteil, Fehlerbeispiele, geforderten Durchsatz, Beleuchtungsvorgaben, PLC/MES-Anforderungen und Rückverfolgbarkeitsziele. Das GYUTRON-Team empfiehlt den passenden Prüfaufbau und Pilotpfad.
seo.contact-sales.title: GYUTRON Vertrieb kontaktieren | Anfrage zur Industrieautomation
seo.contact-sales.desc: Kontaktieren Sie den GYUTRON Vertrieb für industrielle intelligente Hardware, Bildverarbeitung, robuste PDA-Terminals, Sensoren, Prüfgeräte und Unterstützung bei der Automatisierungseinführung.
seo.contact-sales.ogdesc: Teilen Sie Ihre Anforderungen an die industrielle Automatisierung; GYUTRON unterstützt Sie bei Hardware-Stack und Einführungsplanung.
main.308: Sprechen Sie mit GYUTRON über Ihr industrielles Automatisierungsprojekt.
main.309: Teilen Sie Produktinteresse, Standortanforderungen, Zeitplan und Integrationsbedarf. Unser Team unterstützt bei der passenden Hardwarearchitektur für Produktion, Prüfung, Logistik oder Feldeinsatz.
main.313: Nutzen Sie das Formular für Projektdetails oder kontaktieren Sie uns direkt zu Produktspezifikation, Pilotplanung, OEM-/ODM-Programmen und Vertriebspartnerschaften.
main.314: Für eine schnellere Prüfung nennen Sie bitte Produktkategorie, Zielanwendung, erwartete Menge, Land/Region sowie Schnittstellen- oder Zertifizierungsanforderungen.
main.315: Sagen Sie uns, was Sie benötigen
main.317: Senden Sie Ihre Anfrage direkt an GYUTRON. Ein Vertriebsspezialist prüft Ihre Anforderung und meldet sich per E-Mail oder WhatsApp.
main.321: Anwendung, aktueller Prozess, benötigte Funktionen, Zeitplan, Menge, Integrationsschnittstelle oder Zertifizierungsanforderungen.
main.323: Ihre Anfrage wird über das Backend der GYUTRON-Website übermittelt. Falls das Formular nicht verfügbar ist, schreiben Sie an info@gyutron.com oder nutzen Sie WhatsApp.
main.325: Produkttyp, Prüfziel, Datenerfassungsablauf, Umgebung und Integrationsvorgaben definieren.
main.327: Robuste PDA-Terminals, Sensoren, Smart-Kameras, Prüfgeräte, Roboterzellen und Edge-Controller abstimmen.
main.330: Musterprüfung, Pilotkonfiguration, Zubehörauswahl, Dokumentation und Partner-Lieferplanung unterstützen.

sol.common.products.title: Products used in this solution
sol.common.products.intro: Every GYUTRON solution is built from our own hardware. Combine these product lines into one integrated, single-vendor system — sensing, identification, measurement, guidance and actuation from a single partner.

sol.avi.gallery.intro: Representative frames from a deployed cell — the defect classes the system catches and the annotated pass/fail result the line returns. Sample frames shown; real production captures are dropped in per line.

sol.avi.case.title: Inline AOI on an SMT assembly line
sol.avi.case.challenge.v: End-of-line visual checks missed solder and placement defects and depended on operator judgment, letting escapes reach rework.
sol.avi.case.approach.v: Two area-scan stations with controlled lighting inspect every board inline; an integrated code reader ties each defect map to the board 2D code for full traceability.
sol.avi.case.result.v: Every board inspected inline with a consistent, searchable image record per serial number, and far fewer defects escaping to downstream rework.

sol.avi.cases.intro: Inspection scenarios the hardware is built for. Named customer references and on-site photos are added as approvals clear.

sol.measurement-3d-profiling.name: Measurement & 3D Profiling
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.seoTitle: Measurement & 3D Profiling Solutions | Inline Dimensional Gauging & Metrology | GYUTRON
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.seoDesc: Inline dimensional gauging, laser 3D profiling, and traceable metrology from GYUTRON. Sub-micron repeatability, 100% gauging at line speed, gauge-block-traceable results with full SPC logging.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.hero.title: Stop shipping out-of-tolerance parts
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.hero.desc: Hand calipers and sampling miss drift, deform soft parts, and leave no audit trail. GYUTRON gauges every part inline with telecentric optics and laser 3D profiling — height, gap, flatness, and form measured to sub-micron repeatability, traceable to gauge blocks, logged for SPC.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.hero.panelD: 2σ on telecentric optical gauges; laser profile Z-repeatability to 0.5 µm. Traceable to certified gauge blocks; results time-stamped per part for SPC and audit.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.gallery.int: Real captured profiles and gauging overlays from GYUTRON optical and laser measurement systems.

sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cap.intro: Non-contact gauging that replaces manual sampling — dimensional, height, and form data captured on every part, referenced to traceable standards.

sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.title: Sealing-face flatness gauged inline on a machined casting line
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.challeng: A powertrain machining line released castings on a 1-in-30 sample using hand depth gauges. Sealing-face flatness drift between samples reached the customer as leak failures, and contact gauging marked soft aluminum faces.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.approach: A GYUTRON laser profiler scanned each sealing face inline, reconstructing a 3D surface and computing flatness against the datum plane in under 50 ms. Telecentric gauges added bore diameter and bolt-hole spacing in the same cell. All results were referenced to certified gauge blocks and streamed to SPC keyed to the casting serial.
sol.measurement-3d-profiling.cs.result: 100% of faces gauged with 0.6 µm flatness repeatability. Flatness drift now triggers a tool-wear alarm before parts go out of tolerance, leak escapes dropped to near zero, and every casting carries a recallable measurement record.

sol.vision-guided-robotics.hero.desc: Hard-coded fixtures and jigs break the moment parts arrive jumbled, mixed, or unfixtured. GYUTRON gives robots eyes: 2D/3D vision computes the 6-DoF pose of each part in a bin or on a conveyor, plans a collision-free grasp, and streams coordinates to any robot brand. Pick randomly stacked, shiny, and dark parts at production cycle times without re-tooling.

sol.vision-guided-robotics.cap.2.desc: CAD-matched pose: X/Y/Z + roll/pitch/yaw. < 0.1 mm position, < 0.2 deg orientation. Output in robot base frame.

sol.vision-guided-robotics.cap.4.desc: Native drivers for FANUC, ABB, KUKA, Yaskawa, UR, and more. Collision-checked grasps, TCP path, and approach vector.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.title: Capture -> Locate -> Plan -> Guide
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.intro: A deterministic pipeline runs every cycle on a GYUTRON edge controller: image the bin, find each part, plan a reachable grasp, hand coordinates to the robot — closed-loop, under 0.4 s.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.1.title: 1. Capture
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.1.desc: Structured-light 3D head and area-scan camera image the full bin. HDR fusion and noise filtering build a dense, gap-free point cloud of stacked parts.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.2.title: 2. Locate
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.2.desc: CAD/template matching and 3D segmentation isolate each instance and compute its 6-DoF pose. Confidence scoring rejects partial or occluded candidates.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.3.title: 3. Plan
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.3.desc: Grasp ranking selects a reachable, collision-free pick. Bin walls, neighbor parts, gripper geometry, and robot reach are checked before any motion.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.4.title: 4. Guide
sol.vision-guided-robotics.arch.4.desc: Approach, pick, retract, and place coordinates stream to the robot in its base frame. Failed picks trigger automatic re-imaging and the next-best grasp.

sol.vision-guided-robotics.case3.metric: Pick mixed SKUs from totes to conveyor at > 99% success, > 700 picks/hour, vacuum and finger grippers.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.title: Random bin picking of shiny machined parts for CNC loading
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.challenge: A precision machining shop fed CNC lathes by hand from bins of chrome-plated steel shafts dumped in random orientations. The reflective, partially overlapping parts defeated an earlier 2D-only system, and hard tooling could not handle 40+ part variants with frequent changeovers. Manual loading capped uptime and exposed operators to sharp, oily stock.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.approach: A GYUTRON cell paired a blue-light structured-light 3D head with an area-scan camera and a robot-mounted edge controller. HDR multi-exposure capture recovered clean point clouds off the chrome surfaces; CAD matching computed 6-DoF poses, and grasp planning checked bin walls, neighbor parts, and gripper clearance before each pick. Coordinates streamed to the existing robot in its base frame, with automatic re-imaging on any missed grasp.
sol.vision-guided-robotics.cs.result: Sustained > 99.5% first-attempt pick success across the bin, including the last layer, at a 0.38 s locate-and-plan cycle and < 0.1 mm pose accuracy. Changeover to a new part variant dropped from hours of re-fixturing to a CAD import. The cell ran lights-out across two shifts, freeing operators from manual loading and lifting CNC utilization.

sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.title: One mobile cell that sees, decides, picks, and drives
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.intro: Four subsystems integrated as one unit -- 3D vision to see, edge inference to decide, cobot arm to handle, AMR base to move between stations without a fixed footprint.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.1.title: 3D vision + code reading
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.1.desc: Area-scan and 3D heads locate parts at +/-0.05 mm; on-arm code reader confirms 1D/2D/DPM identity at 99.5% first pass.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.2.title: Edge decision-making
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.2.desc: On-board controller runs pose estimation and grasp planning; no PC in the loop, <150 ms from capture to motion command.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.3.title: Cobot handling
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.3.desc: 6-axis collaborative arm, force-limited for shared space; +/-0.02 mm repeatability, tool-change for grippers and probes.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.4.title: Autonomous mobility
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cap.4.desc: SLAM-guided AMR base docks at +/-100 m and 1 deg; safety-rated lidar, no floor magnets or tape, IP54 chassis.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.eyebrow: The full integrated stack, one vendor
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.title: AMR base to fleet-and-MES, no integration seams
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.intro: GYUTRON builds every layer -- mobile base, arm, 3D vision, code reading, and the fleet/MES link -- so pose, identity, and motion share one clock with no protocol mismatch between subsystems.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.1.title: AMR base
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.1.desc: SLAM navigation and traffic management move the cell between stations; docks at +/-100 m, 1 deg before the arm engages.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.2.title: Cobot arm
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.2.desc: Force-limited 6-axis arm executes the pick-place; +/-0.02 mm repeatability, tool-change for grippers, nozzles, and probes.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.3.title: 3D vision
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.3.desc: Area-scan and 3D heads capture the scene on arrival; 6-DoF pose at +/-0.05 mm corrects for AMR docking offset.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.4.title: Code reading
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.4.desc: On-arm reader verifies 1D/2D/DPM identity at 99.5% first pass; wrong-part and wrong-slot picks rejected before release.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.5.title: Fleet & MES
sol.composite-mobile-robots.arch.5.desc: Edge controller reports pose, read result, and AMR position to fleet manager and MES per cycle for live traceability.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.title: How we deploy it
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.intro: A staged path from your floor plan and parts to a calibrated, MES-linked mobile cell on your line.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.1.title: Map & scope
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.1.desc: Walk the layout, capture AMR routes and dock points, score graspability and read rate on your sample parts.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.2.title: Calibrate & integrate
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.2.desc: Hand-eye and base-to-tool calibration, gripper and reach validation, fieldbus and MES link to your line.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.3.title: Run & monitor
sol.composite-mobile-robots.wf.3.desc: Fleet dashboards for throughput, read rate, and pick success; re-grasp and route logic tuned to takt.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cases.title: Where the mobile cell runs

sol.composite-mobile-robots.case1.metric: AMR shuttles reels and trays between feeders; 3D-guided load, code-verified to MES, <8 s per pick-and-place

sol.composite-mobile-robots.case3.metric: Mixed-SKU totes located in 3D, picked to bin floor, barcode-confirmed at 99.5% first pass

sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.title: Mobile manipulators feeding 3C and semiconductor lines
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.challenge: Reels, trays, and carriers move between SMT feeders, test handlers, and stockers by manual cart. Operators walk parts station to station, mis-loads stop the line, and there is no per-carrier record of who touched what or when.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.approach: A mobile manipulator -- AMR base, cobot arm, 3D area-scan head, and on-arm code reader -- drives to each station and docks at +/-100 m. The 3D head locates the carrier and corrects for docking offset; the arm picks to +/-0.05 mm. The on-arm reader confirms the DPM or 2D code before release, and the edge controller reports pose, identity, and AMR position to MES every cycle.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cs.result: Hands-free transfer between tools, <8 s per pick-and-place, 99.5% first-pass code read, and zero wrong-slot loads released. Full chain-of-custody per carrier; one fleet tends multiple lines instead of operators walking parts.
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cta.title: Bring us your station layout and takt time
sol.composite-mobile-robots.cta.desc: We'll map AMR routes, validate reach and grasp on your parts, and quote a cell that hits your cycle target -- or run a feasibility study on your line.
